LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT BAYWEST CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF ANY CONTRACT COMPLETED BEFORE ITS LICENSE WAS SUSPENDED. OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2010 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set 1, Form Interrogatories, Set 3, Reuqest For Production Of Documents, Set 2
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One; Form Interrogatories, Set 3; Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to Request For Admission Nos. 1, 2, 3, 27, 37, 39, 45, 47, and 52 as overly broad and irrelevant.
The motion is granted as to Request For Admission Nos. 4, 5, 6, 7, 8-26, 28-36, 38, 40-44, 47-51, and 53-60.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to Request For Admission Nos. 4, 5, 6, 7, 8-26, 28-36, 38, 40-44, 47-51, and 53-60. The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to Request For Admission Nos. 1, 2, 3, 27, 37, 39, 45, 47, and 52.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production No. 22.
Further responses shall be made within 7 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- SARKIS KHANZADIAN, TEL. NO. 786-2307, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT BAYWEST CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF ANY CONTRACT COMPLETED BEFORE ITS LICENSE WAS SUSPENDED. OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Discovery Requests;
Off Calendar Feb-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Sarkis Khanzadian's Motion To Compel Response To Discovery Requests is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Sanctions are denied. Plaintiff has not submitted a declaration supporting an amount of sanctions. Additionally, as Plaintiff is pro per, he has not incurred attorneys fees, and he would not be entitled to sanctions. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
